question.content: In plant salt-stress conditions, Na+ can move upward through the xylem, while Nax1 and Nax2 retrieve Na+ from xylem into surrounding cells. When Nax1 and Nax2 transporter level or activity increases, determine whether Na+ accumulation in cells around the xylem increases or decreases. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Use ASCII characters only. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Set a simplified transport geometry with xylem upward flow and surrounding cells.
step_1.key_point: Separate xylem flow from retrieval into nearby cells.

step_3.content: Add Nax1 and Nax2 retrieval paths from xylem to cells around the xylem wall.
step_3.key_point: Nax-mediated retrieval redirects Na+ into surrounding cells.

step_5.content: Increase Nax1/Nax2 activity and compare ion distribution between xylem stream and surrounding cells.
step_5.key_point: Higher retrieval gives more Na+ in surrounding cells and less upward Na+ transport.
